evals.prompt: Audit the ux-discovery-v2 plan at docs/plans/drafted/ux-discovery-v2/. I want to know if it's ready to activate or if there are issues to fix before we hand it to opencode.
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report saved to docs/plans/drafted/ux-discovery-v2/audit/ with: frontmatter verdict, blocking/advisory issues tables with specific evidence, prior art compliance section, gate coverage table, and a clear verdict with a one-sentence reason.
assertions: Audit file is written to the plan folder (docs/plans/drafted/ux-discovery-v2/audit/) — not just printed to stdout
Output contains YAML frontmatter with a 'verdict' field set to READY, NEEDS REVISION, or NOT READY
Prior art section references the expired ui-ux-discovery plan and mentions at least one specific prior finding (e.g., 269 raw hex values, 48 dead CSS classes, or token inventory)
Gate coverage table or section is present and correctly marks all 3 discovery sprints as N/A for code-modifying gates (because no source code is changed)
Session start compliance is assessed — output mentions whether each sprint has a build-current-state.sh step
Any scripts referenced in the sprint files are verified against the actual scripts/agent/ directory contents (not assumed to exist)
Blocking issues section is present — either lists specific issues with evidence and fix, or explicitly states zero blocking issues with a reason

evals.prompt: Codex, review the frontend-discovery-v2 sprint FE2-R1 — specifically whether the dep-cruiser and code-index steps are actually executable as written. Write your audit so Claude can decide whether to activate it.
evals.expected_output: A sprint-level audit that verifies: npm run validate:architecture exists in package.json, scripts/agent/code-query.sh exists and supports the subcommands used, find/wc/awk commands are syntactically correct, and the output format section produces verifiable output.
assertions: Audit checks whether 'npm run validate:architecture' exists by reading package.json — not by assuming
Audit checks whether scripts/agent/code-query.sh exists using a file system check (ls or find)
Audit assesses whether code-query.sh supports the subcommands called in the sprint (component, consumers, or similar)
Verdict is specific to FE2-R1 (not a verdict on the entire plan)
Audit file is written to disk (docs/plans/drafted/frontend-discovery-v2/audit/ or the workspace output path)
Audit does not flag architectural design decisions as issues — stays within sprint feasibility scope

evals.prompt: Is folder-structure-v2 ready to be activated? Give me a proper audit I can use to make the decision.
evals.expected_output: An audit that correctly returns NOT READY, correctly identifies the stub state as intentional (not a deficiency), names the 3 dependency plans that must complete first, and states the exact condition for readiness.
assertions: Audit correctly identifies that the lack of sprint files is intentional — documented in the README as 'DO NOT EXECUTE' — not treated as a bug or omission
Audit names all 3 dependency plans: ux-discovery-v2, frontend-discovery-v2, backend-discovery-v2
Audit states the activation condition: sprint files can be written only after all discovery outputs are available
Audit reads the prior art (expired/src-structure-refactor) and notes inherited decisions still valid
Audit file is written to docs/plans/drafted/folder-structure-v2/audit/ or the workspace output path
